title: SpaceX acquires AI startup Cursor for $60 billion
summary: Elon Musk's company buys Cursor to boost xAI, in a deal that combines shares after its IPO.

title: Snap launches its SPECS augmented reality glasses for $2,195
summary: Snap's glasses are now available for pre-order, offering an advanced augmented reality experience.

title: Android 17 is now available with multitasking features and Gemini improvements
summary: Google releases the new version of its operating system for Pixel devices, with artificial intelligence integration.

title: SpaceX goes public with a valuation exceeding $2.6 trillion
summary: Elon Musk's company debuts on the stock market and temporarily surpasses Amazon in market capitalization.

title: Threads reaches 500 million monthly users and adds new features
summary: Meta's social network implements privacy controls and communities, consolidating its growth.

title: Pepephone activates 24-hour customer service but outsources its call center
summary: The operator offers continuous support but sparks controversy by withdrawing its commitment to keep the center in Spain.

title: Simyo launches fiber for second homes and improves its mobile rates
summary: The operator expands its fiber offering for holiday homes with reduced prices and more gigs.

title: Haier presents its new range of MiniLED TVs and giant screens in Spain
summary: The Chinese brand bets on MiniLED technology and large sizes to compete in the Spanish market.

title: Robinhood lays off 10% of its workforce as part of a restructuring
summary: The trading platform cuts staff to flatten its organizational structure, according to the company.

title: Wolfram Language and Mathematica 15 are now available
summary: The new version of the computational language includes significant improvements in mathematical and programming capabilities.

title: DOJ defends xAI's gas turbines on national security grounds in NAACP lawsuit
summary: The U.S. Department of Justice argues that the unauthorized turbines are essential for national security.

title: Anthropic makes an enemy of the Trump administration, but sales suggest it could benefit
summary: Despite the political conflict, sales data indicate a possible positive impact for the AI startup.

title: India temporarily bans Telegram over exam fraud
summary: The Indian government blocks the app while investigating its use in academic frauds.

title: A vulnerability in FIFA's internal system allowed modification of television broadcasts
summary: Anyone with access could alter the World Cup TV signal, according to an investigation.

title: Rivian lays off hundreds of employees after start of R2 deliveries
summary: The electric vehicle company cuts staff to reduce costs while increasing production.

title: CISA alerts about a critical vulnerability in Joomla JCE actively exploited
summary: The flaw allows remote PHP code execution and has already been exploited in attacks.

title: A flaw in the Vertex AI SDK allows hijacking model loads
summary: Researchers discover that attackers can exploit the vulnerability to take control of AI models.

title: 144 Mastra npm packages compromised after hijacking of a contributor account
summary: A supply chain attack infects multiple packages in the npm registry.

title: Shareholders sue Microsoft for spending too much on AI, causing its stock to crash
summary: The class-action lawsuit accuses Microsoft of artificially inflating its stock price with excessive investments in artificial intelligence.

title: Forty experts lock themselves in to prevent artificial intelligence from collapsing society
summary: A group of specialists debates how to mitigate the catastrophic risks of AI in a secret meeting.
